name: El Molar, Colmenar Viejo, Guadalix de la Sierra, El Vellón – circular route
name_original: El Molar, Colmenar Viejo, Guadalix de la Sierra, El Vellón – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around El Molar offer diverse terrain within the Sierra Norte de Madrid, characterized by varied landscapes including mountain passes, river valleys, and reservoirs. The region features roads with low traffic, making it suitable for road cyclists. Elevations range from gentle gradients to significant climbs, with routes passing through areas with pine groves, oak groves, and holm oaks. The area's physical features include the Jarama and Guadalix rivers, as well as reservoirs like Pedrezuela and Atazar. Best road cycling routes around El Molar The most popular road cycling route is El Molar, Colmenar Viejo, Guadalix de la Sierra, El Vellón – circular route, a 45.3 miles (72.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Molar, El Vellon, Torrelaguna, El Berrueco - circular route, a difficult 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and descents through the region's rural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Santillana Reservoir – Alto del Cerro de San Pedro loop from El Molar, a 47.4 miles (76.3 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the Santillana Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around El Molar is defined by mountain passes, river valleys, and reservoirs, with routes traversing diverse forest types. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult climbs. The routes in El Molar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 7 reviews. More than 2,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Molar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around El Molar?
A: El Molar, situated in the Sierra Norte de Madrid, offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll encounter varied terrain including mountain passes, river valleys, and areas around reservoirs. Routes traverse different forest types such as pine groves, oak groves, and holm oaks, providing a mix of gradients from gentle to challenging climbs.

Q: Are there many road cycling routes available in El Molar?
A: Yes, komoot offers over 10 road cycling routes around El Molar. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from moderate rides to difficult climbs, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What are some of the most scenic natural features I can see while cycling in El Molar?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle along the Jarama and Guadalix rivers, and past reservoirs like the Pedrezuela reservoir. A notable natural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadalix River, a beautiful double waterfall nestled in a rocky canyon.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in El Molar?
A: Yes, many routes around El Molar are circular. For instance, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977453360/el-molar-colmenar-viejo-guadalix-de-la-sierra-el-vellon-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Molar, Colmenar Viejo, Guadalix de la Sierra, El Vellón – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977042179/el-molar-el-vellon-torrelaguna-el-berrueco-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Molar, El Vellon, Torrelaguna, El Berrueco - circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers challenging climbs through rural landscapes.

Q: What kind of difficulty levels can I expect from road cycling routes in El Molar?
A: The road cycling routes around El Molar offer a good mix of challenges. You'll find several moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31639768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pedrezuela Reservoir – Pedrezuela Reservoir loop from El Molar&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 34 km long. There are also more difficult routes with significant elevation gains, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from El Molar&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1600 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in El Molar?
A: The road cycling routes in El Molar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from numerous reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the low traffic on many roads, and the beautiful natural surroundings of the Sierra Norte de Madrid.

Q: Are there any interesting cultural or historical landmarks to see near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, El Molar and its surroundings offer unique attractions. You can visit the historic underground wine cellars known as 'Cuevas' in El Molar itself. Nearby, the town of Buitrago del Lozoya, often part of cycling itineraries, features historic walls, a charming old town, and even a Picasso Museum.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Molar?
A: El Molar is suitable for road cycling for much of the year. The region's climate generally allows for pleasant riding conditions, though it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts, especially when planning rides with significant elevation changes in the mountainous areas.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the region's reservoirs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31806217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir – Alto del Cerro de San Pedro loop from El Molar&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the area surrounding the Santillana Reservoir. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find places to stop for refreshments or a meal along the routes?
A: El Molar itself is known for its 'Cuevas,' historic underground wine cellars now used as bars and dining rooms, offering a unique spot for a mid-route snack or meal. The rural towns and villages along the routes in the Sierra Norte de Madrid often provide opportunities for refreshments.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain and varied landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Madrid provide several opportunities for scenic viewpoints. One notable spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex with Views of the Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding mountain ranges.
tips: Take a moment to appreciate the beautiful views from this place.

Soto is one of the epicenters of cycling in the Sierra de Guadarrama. From here you can access many roads and tracks that lead to the mountain itself. Its leisure and restaurant offerings have made it the origin and end of long days by bike and beers and after-meals reminiscing about the adventure.
There are two clearly differentiated urban centers, a beautiful watchtower of Arab origin, lots of traditional architecture and Los Quebraones, a very interesting natural area.

One of the accesses to the Madrid mountains, has an altitude of 850 meters. El Molar is known for its caves, which number more than two hundred. Some are of medieval and Muslim origin. In many of them, the wines produced in the town mature. Others have been used for the installation of restaurants, with their typical dishes such as blood sausage soup, sheep stew or lamb with salt and garlic.
